title: heracleum sphondylium subsp. chloranthum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium subsp. chloranthum, also known as wild angelica or hog's fennel, is a subspecies of perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Heracleum sphondylium subsp. chloranthum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to make teas, syrups, and ointments that can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
